frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Value-based BEA extended with temporally correlated, drifting evidence samples

frameworks.name: value construction
frameworks.one_line: Subjective values are built during deliberation, not retrieved as stable estimates

frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: Standard framework for choice + RT in perceptual and value decisions

core_question: Do subjective values drift across a value-based choice session, and can within-session revaluation be recovered from choice patterns?
key_assumptions: Standard BEA assumes temporally uncorrelated evidence noise
Subjective value depends on internal state (desires, attention, goals) that can change
Choice + RT patterns are informative about latent value changes
manipulations: Reanalysis of an established two-alternative snack-preference task
Comparison of stated values vs Reval-inferred dynamic values
BEA fits with independent vs temporally correlated evidence samples
key_findings: Reval recovers significant within-session changes in item value over ~1 hour
Reval-derived values predict choices and response times better than stated values
Reval values better explain vmPFC BOLD signal than stated values
A BEA variant with correlated successive samples reproduces revaluation
interpretations: Revaluation reflects value construction during deliberation rather than retrieval of stable utilities
Temporally correlated evidence is a more appropriate noise model for value-based BEA than independent samples
